quotes.quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.
quotes.rationale: Literature-similarity filter discards ideas, gating passage from ideation to execution.

quotes.quote: After the completion of each experiment, Aider is then given the results and told to take notes in the style of an experimental journal. Currently, it only conditions on text but in future versions, this could include data visualizations or any modality. Conditional on the results, it then re-plans and implements the next experiment. This process is repeated up to five times.
quotes.rationale: Results condition re-planning of the next experiment, i.e. implemented search control with journal notes as memory.

quotes.quote: To evaluate the LLM-based reviewer’s performance, we compared the artificially generated decisions with ground truth data for 500 ICLR 2022 papers extracted from the publicly available OpenReview dataset (Berto, 2024).
quotes.rationale: Reviewer decisions benchmarked against human ground-truth decisions.

quotes.quote: Finally, The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.
quotes.rationale: Reviewer feedback is admitted to the archive, a memory-update path beyond terminal scoring.

quotes.quote: Review ensembling (5 reviews) and subsequent meta-aggregation, on the other hand, did not affect the reviewer’s performance, but can reduce variance.
quotes.rationale: Reported null effect of ensembling qualifies the reliability finding.

quotes.quote: Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.
quotes.rationale: Stated modality limitation of the reviewer channel.

quotes.quote: Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.
quotes.rationale: Explicitly an unimplemented but proposed extension of the reviewer.

diagnostics.consequence: Ideas may be discarded or admitted by an unvalidated novelty judgment, and reviewer benchmark results cannot be transferred to this gate.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.

To evaluate the LLM-based reviewer’s performance, we compared the artificially generated decisions with ground truth data for 500 ICLR 2022 papers extracted from the publicly available OpenReview dataset (Berto, 2024).

diagnostics.statement: The novelty filter that gates ideas into execution has no reported reliability study of its own; the reported benchmark validation applies only to the terminal paper reviewer.

diagnostics.consequence: Quality judgments routed into the archive rest on text-only, non-interactive assessment whose accuracy is not improved by aggregation.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.
Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.
Review ensembling (5 reviews) and subsequent meta-aggregation, on the other hand, did not affect the reviewer’s performance, but can reduce variance.

diagnostics.statement: The reviewer channel operates without figure comprehension and without rebuttal interaction, and ensembling did not improve its performance.

diagnostics.consequence: Closed computational feedback drives search on self-generated signals whose correctness is not independently established, leaving human follow-up necessary.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: More generally, we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value. Instead, we advise treating generated papers as hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on.

After the completion of each experiment, Aider is then given the results and told to take notes in the style of an experimental journal. Currently, it only conditions on text but in future versions, this could include data visualizations or any modality. Conditional on the results, it then re-plans and implements the next experiment. This process is repeated up to five times.

diagnostics.statement: The authors state the system's scientific content should not be taken at face value, so the internal loop does not establish empirical adequacy of the findings it archives.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: After idea generation, we filter ideas by connecting the language model with the Semantic Scholar API (Fricke, 2018) and web access as a tool (Schick et al., 2024). This allows The AI Scientist to discard any idea that is too similar to existing literature.

recommendations.proposed_action: Validate the literature-similarity ideation filter against human novelty labels and report its own discrimination performance rather than relying on the reviewer benchmark.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Filter accuracy exceeds a stated baseline such as keyword similarity matching
success_check.evidence_needed: Published discrimination study covering the ideation filter alone
success_check.observable: Agreement between filter discard decisions and human novelty judgments on a labelled idea set

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Finally, since it does not currently use any vision capabilities, The AI Scientist (including the reviewer) is unable to view figures and must rely on textual descriptions of them.
Unlike standard reviewers, the automated reviewer is unable to ask questions to the authors in a rebuttal phase, although this could readily be incorporated into our framework.

recommendations.proposed_action: Add figure-aware review and the described rebuttal exchange to the automated reviewer, then re-run the 500-paper ground-truth comparison to test whether these limitations drive residual error.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Post-change agreement exceeds the current text-only configuration
success_check.evidence_needed: Paired re-run of the same benchmark under both configurations
success_check.observable: Reviewer/ground-truth agreement before and after adding vision and rebuttal capability

exact_quotes.exact_quote: More generally, we do not recommend taking the scientific content of this version of The AI Scientist at face value. Instead, we advise treating generated papers as hints of promising ideas for practitioners to follow up on.
Finally, The AI Scientist adds the completed ideas and reviewer feedback to its archive of scientific findings, and the process repeats.

recommendations.proposed_action: Mark archived findings with an independent verification status before they re-enter the loop, so unverified content the authors caution against is separated from checked results.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: All archived entries labelled verified or unverified
success_check.evidence_needed: Archive schema documentation and an audit of a sample of archived entries
success_check.observable: Proportion of archived findings carrying an explicit verification status

memory_structure: experimental journal notes conditioned on results
archive of completed ideas and reviewer feedback
policy_type: LLM-driven iterative experiment re-planning loop capped at five iterations
literature-similarity gating of generated ideas before execution
